confirmed.summary: FR-005 is framed as 'promote/move' a script that does not exist in this repo — it must be authored from scratch
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:14 & FR-005 (spec.md:43) say 'move work/snippets/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh -> scripts/ci/'. Verified absent: `test -e` -> MISSING; `git log --all -- **/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh` empty; the whole `work/` tree is gitignored (.gitignore:184) and does not exist on disk. scripts/ci/ contains only quality_gate_decision.py. The script lives only on maintainer branch 'design/pr-landing-followups-and-sonar-remediation', which is not present here.
confirmed.recommendation: Rescope FR-005/US3 from 'relocate a tracked-elsewhere script' to 'author a read-only SonarCloud REST tool' covering the four stated capabilities (quality-gate status, coverage metrics, per-file uncovered lines, issues-by-rule/file) plus arg-parsing, error handling, and a smoke test. Either import Stijn's actual script into the mission inputs before planning, or size the tasks for authoring — not a one-line file move. Update SC-003 accordingly.

verdict.reasoning:
Finding withstands adversarial verification against the live repo. Verified: (1) spec.md:43 (FR-005) literally frames the work as "move work/snippets/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh -> scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh", and spec.md:14/25 describe it as an already-implemented but gitignored/untracked script; (2) test -e -> MISSING and the whole work/ tree is absent from disk; (3) the filename appears in ZERO trees/commits across ALL refs (verified via exact pathspec, --name-only grep, and a per-ref ls-tree scan of every for-each-ref) — it is nowhere in git history; (4) git check-ignore confirms .gitignore:184 (`work`) ignores the path (also /work/ at 114/158); (5) scripts/ci/ contains only quality_gate_decision.py; (6) the mission dossier carries no imported copy (research/, checklists/ empty; tasks/ only .gitkeep+README). Therefore FR-005/US3, plus dependents FR-006 (smoke test), SC-003, and C-003 (shellcheck/ruff/mypy gate), are anchored to a source artifact that does not exist in this repo, in any reachable ref, or in mission inputs — the honest work is authoring a read-only SonarCloud REST tool (4 capabilities + arg-parsing + error handling + token-free smoke test) or importing the file out-of-band, not a git mv. The recommendation is correct.

The finding contains one factual slip: it claims the maintainer branch design/pr-landing-followups-and-sonar-remediation is "not present here", but it IS present as remotes/stijn/design/... (remote stijn -> stijn-dejongh/spec-kitty.git). Critically, the script is NOT tracked on that branch either (work/ is gitignored there too), so it exists only as an untracked working-copy file on the maintainer's machine. This error understates the problem rather than undermining it: the artifact is un-fetchable via git anywhere, so it cannot be cherry-picked/moved and must be handed over or rewritten.

Severity adjusted high -> medium: genuine, material sizing/feasibility gap, but on a P2/Medium-priority deliverable (not the P1 projectVersion work), caught at spec stage before any tasks were authored (tasks/ empty) so zero downstream effort mis-sized yet and the fix is a cheap pre-/tasks rescope; the spec also already signposts the script as gitignored/untracked. High is defensible given cross-fork un-fetchability; medium is the calibrated call.

confirmed.summary: FR-005 promotes a source file that does not exist in this workspace or on any git ref
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:43 FR-005 'move work/snippets/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh -> scripts/ci/...'; spec.md:14 asserts it 'already implements the needed SonarCloud REST calls'. Repo proof: .gitignore:184 ignores work/; `git log --all -- **/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh` is empty; the file is NOT on remotes/stijn/design/pr-landing-followups-and-sonar-remediation; `work/` dir is absent from the checkout.
confirmed.recommendation: A 'move X -> Y' task is unexecutable when X is absent and untracked. Either embed the script's full contents/behavioral contract in the spec (required subcommands, exact args, output shape for quality-gate status / coverage metrics / per-file uncovered lines / issues-by-rule-file), or add an explicit provenance step naming where the implementer obtains the maintainer's local copy. As written, FR-005/SC-003 cannot be started from this workspace.

verdict.reasoning:
All claimed evidence verified against the live repo. spec.md:43 FR-005 instructs "move work/snippets/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh -> scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh"; spec.md:14 asserts it already implements the REST calls but is gitignored/untracked. .gitignore:184 is literally `work` (also /work/ at 114, 158). The file is absent from the filesystem (work/ dir does not exist, no *branch_review* file anywhere), untracked (git ls-files empty), and never added on any ref: git log --all --diff-filter=A is empty and an exhaustive git rev-list --all | git ls-tree grep finds nothing. Critically, it is NOT on the cited provenance branch stijn/design/pr-landing-followups-and-sonar-remediation (tip 5a5e903 resolves; file absent), nor on main, nor on the mission's own feat/ and kitty/ branches. So FR-005 references a source artifact that provably does not exist in this workspace or on any reachable ref, and the spec's own provenance chain to obtain it is broken.

The finding survives the strongest refutation ("spec already self-discloses untracked status"): 'gitignored/untracked' implies present-but-uncommitted, whereas the file is wholly absent and unrecoverable via the documented provenance, so a faithful implementer following FR-005/Key Entities dead-ends. The recommendation (embed the behavioral contract or name the provenance) is the correct remediation.

Severity adjusted high->medium: the defect blocks only the P2 third (US3/FR-005/FR-006/SC-003); the P1 core (US1 projectVersion, FR-001/002) and US2 coverage doc are independently executable. The intent is recoverable because the spec supplies a behavioral sketch (read-only; quality-gate status, coverage metrics, per-file uncovered lines, issues-by-rule/file; no mutation) plus the FR-003 API endpoint, and FR-006 permits a recorded fixture — so the tool can be authored fresh; only the literal "move X->Y" phrasing is unexecutable. Real completeness/executability defect, but medium given recoverable intent and P2 blast radius. (Note: the spec's sibling claim is accurate — scripts/ci/quality_gate_decision.py does exist.)

confirmed.summary: SC-001 acceptance is unverifiable at PR/CI time for this cross-fork draft
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:67 SC-001 'A SonarCloud analysis after the fix reports a real projectVersion ... verifiable via the promoted tool against the public API.' But ci-quality.yml:2843 gates the whole sonarcloud job on `secrets.SONAR_TOKEN`, and the scan (ci-quality.yml:3130-3138) and quality-gate steps only run when enabled==true. meta.json:11 marks this a 'Cross-fork draft' — fork PRs receive no secrets, so no new analysis runs; the read API keeps returning the pre-fix projectVersion until a token-bearing analysis runs post-merge. Baseline-reset ('no longer anchored at 2026-03-21') is server-side and post-merge.
confirmed.recommendation: Split SC-001 into (a) a statically verifiable criterion checkable in the diff/CI now — projectVersion is wired into the Materialize step and/or scanner args and appears in the printed effective sonar-project.properties — and (b) a clearly-labelled post-merge observation that baseline reset is confirmed on SonarCloud after the first token-bearing analysis. Do not gate acceptance on state that cannot be observed at review time.

verdict.reasoning: All load-bearing evidence verified against live artifacts. spec.md:67 SC-001 text matches verbatim, including the "verifiable via the promoted tool against the public API" clause. ci-quality.yml:2843 is the SONAR_TOKEN gate; the Scan (3129-3139) and Quality Gate (3141-3147) steps are gated on enabled=='true'. meta.json:11 declares "Cross-fork draft"; spec Status is Draft. The core technical claim holds and is actually understated: the entire sonarcloud job carries a job-level if (ci-quality.yml:2837) restricting it to schedule/workflow_dispatch, so a fresh analysis never runs on pull_request at all — token or not. A fork PR additionally receives no secrets. Both converge on the finding's conclusion: no fresh analysis at PR/CI/review time, so the promoted tool reading the public API returns the last analysis's projectVersion ("not provided") and the 2026-03-21 baseline until a token-bearing analysis runs post-merge (nightly/dispatch on the base repo). projectVersion is currently wired nowhere (sonar-project.properties, Materialize step, scanner args all lack it), and the review-time staleness persists even after the fix is implemented — so SC-001 as worded conflates a post-merge server-side observation with a review-time check. Not already handled: the dossier is spec-only (no plan/tasks/acceptance doc; checklists/research empty). Steel-manning fails: the "SC are maintainer-verified" defense is defeated by SC-001 explicitly claiming review-time verifiability; the finding's omission of the event-gate under-claims rather than over-claims. One minor wart in the recommendation (the "printed effective sonar-project.properties" sub-clause is itself only emitted when the token-gated job runs, hence not observable at PR time), but the primary static-diff path (projectVersion in Materialize/scanner-args diff) is sound, so the problem statement stands. Severity adjusted from high to medium: it is a genuine, non-cosmetic testability issue with a real acceptance-friction failure mode (stale read could wrongly block/reject a correct fix), but it is a spec-wording clarity problem with a trivial remedy (split SC-001 into a static-now check + a labelled post-merge observation); no code defect, security, data, or risk-of-accepting-broken-work dimension, which keeps it below high.

dropped.summary: US2/FR-004 are premised on a 'PR SonarCloud signal' that CI never produces — Sonar skips PRs entirely
dropped.why_refuted: The finding's factual mechanics are correct (verified live): the sonarcloud job runs only on schedule/workflow_dispatch (ci-quality.yml:2837, comment "PRs skip Sonar entirely" at 2789, "Nightly Sonar run on the default branch" at 66), there is no sonar.pullrequest.* decoration, and diff-coverage is PR-only (ci-quality.yml:2664, "PR-only diff coverage gate" at 2638). So Sonar whole-repo coverage and diff-coverage never render on the same PR, and spec.md:9's literal phrase "reading a PR's SonarCloud signal" is imprecise. However, as FRAMED the finding does not withstand scrutiny and defaults to real=false: (1) Its headline claim that US2/FR-004 are "premised on" a nonexistent PR Sonar signal is an overstatement — US2 (spec.md:21) rests on interpretability of two coverage numbers and FR-004 (spec.md:42) on doc discoverability; neither depends on both numbers appearing on the same page. The whole-repo-vs-diff confusion is real regardless of render location, so US2/FR-004 are not invalidated. (2) The recommendation to drop the "PR reviewer" audience and re-anchor to the Sonar dashboard is partly wrong/harmful: the PR reviewer is exactly who sees the PR-only diff-coverage gate and gets confused vs the dashboard number, and a repo doc cannot be linked from SonarCloud's own dashboard UI — FR-004 already correctly anchors to docs/ + "referenced from the coverage/quality-gate context" (the controllable PR/CI surface). (3) The finding quotes "side-by-side on a PR" as inaccurate framing to remove, but that phrase does not exist in the spec (grep NOT FOUND); the spec says "next to," meaning cognitive comparison. (4) Severity/category is inflated: it is neither a boundary/contract violation nor high-impact — all FRs, SCs, and the deliverable (doc in docs/, promoted read-only script) are technically sound and unaffected, and no WP/task artifact exists that depends on a PR Sonar signal (tasks/ is empty boilerplate). The only surviving issue is a one-line wording imprecision in spec.md:9, a low-severity editorial nit, not a high boundary defect that premises US2/FR-004 on a nonexistent signal.

dropped.summary: SC-001/US1 cannot be verified within the mission lifecycle; acceptance is post-merge and out-of-band
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's only correct element is the sub-fact that the `sonarcloud` job gate (`ci-quality.yml:2837`, `if: always() && (github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')`, plus the "PRs skip Sonar entirely" comment at 2788-2790) means Sonar does not run on `pull_request`/`push`. Everything the finding builds on top of that fails scrutiny.

(1) Strawman: the spec never presents SC-001/US1 as branch-/PR-testable. SC-001 (spec.md:67) explicitly says "verifiable via the promoted tool against the public API" and US1's test (spec.md:18) says "after the fix, a SonarCloud analysis reports a real projectVersion." Both are already framed as observational, out-of-band queries via the promoted read-only tool (the whole purpose of US3/FR-005). The finding's recommendation ("mark SC-001 as observational, query via the tool against the public API") is essentially already the spec's wording.

(2) Over-stated technical claim: "the change cannot be observed before merge" is false. `workflow_dispatch` (the second arm of the cited gate) is not restricted to the default branch; a maintainer can dispatch ci-quality.yml on the mission branch, and the `sonar.branch.name` step (line 3121, `github.head_ref || github.ref_name`) resolves to that branch, making the projectVersion wiring observable pre-merge. Only `schedule` is default-branch-bound (that restriction is at the cron comment line 66, not line 2837, which the finding mis-attributes as the source of "on the default branch").

(3) Premature: the mission is at spec stage only (status.events.jsonl shows MissionCreated + SpecifyStarted; no plan.md/tasks.md/acceptance doc). There is no acceptance plan to be deficient, and the mission's actual in-lifecycle deliverables (FR-001/002 config wiring, FR-005/006 tracked tool + tokenless smoke test in CI, FR-003/004 doc) are all verifiable within the lifecycle; SC-001's server-side SonarCloud confirmation is inherently downstream and the spec says so.

The finding conflates a known, benign property of Sonar analysis with a spec defect, attacks language the spec doesn't contain, and mis-states that pre-merge observation is impossible. It does not identify a real gap. Severity adjusted to low: the residual kernel (noting the dispatch-or-nightly confirmation path in a future acceptance plan) is a minor documentation nicety already covered by SC-001's "verifiable via the promoted tool against the public API."

dropped.summary: FR-001's effect on the baseline hinges on an unseen SonarCloud 'New Code = Previous version' server-side setting not listed as an assumption
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's core premise — that the SonarCloud "New Code = Previous version" mode is an unseen, unverified server-side setting that could instead be "Number of days"/"Reference branch," rendering FR-001 a silent no-op — is refuted by live in-repo evidence on four independent axes.

(1) The mode is documented for the exact project. kitty-specs/quality-devex-hardening-3-2-01KRJGKH/research.md §2 states, for the Priivacy-ai_spec-kitty project (same sonar.projectKey as sonar-project.properties): "The current 'previous version' baseline anchors the new_coverage... metrics" and "New-code window: anchored to the previous version baseline (Sonar default). Exact baseline tag visible in the Sonar Priivacy-ai_spec-kitty project's Administration → New Code panel." That is direct confirmation of the very setting the finding claims is unverified.

(2) The spec grounding already names the mode: spec.md:12 attributes the frozen baseline to "freezing the previous_version new-code baseline" — an explicit mode reference, not an unexamined assumption.

(3) The symptom falsifies the finding's alternative modes. The documented pathology — a fixed 2026-03-21 anchor accumulating "~4 months of undifferentiated drift," stated as "confirmed against the live repo" (spec.md:9,11,18) — is the signature of "Previous version" mode with a static projectVersion. "Number of days" produces a rolling window (no fixed anchor); "Reference branch" compares to a branch (no fixed calendar date). The alternatives are inconsistent with the observed, live-confirmed symptom, so they are already falsified, not open risks.

(4) The "silent no-op" risk is foreclosed by design. SC-001 (spec.md:67) is explicitly verifiable ("the new-code baseline is no longer anchored at 2026-03-21 — verifiable via the promoted tool against the public API"), and US3/FR-012 (spec.md:25) exists precisely so the projectVersion verification (SC-001) routes through the canonical read-only tool. The verification step the finding "recommends adding" is already a deliverable, and SC-001 requires observable baseline movement — a no-op fails SC-001 rather than passing silently.

Residual: the only literally-true fragment is that the formal Assumptions block (spec.md:77-79) does not enumerate "New Code = Previous version" as a named line. That is a trivial documentation-completeness nit, already largely discharged by grounding line 12 and the corroborating sibling research; it does not rise to medium/integration and does not support the "silent no-op" thesis. Verdict: not real; at most a low-value doc nit.

dropped.summary: FR-003/C-002/edge-case decision logic can mis-triage the intentional file-set difference into an NFR-002-violating Sonar sources narrowing
dropped.why_refuted: Evidence anchors are all factually accurate against live artifacts: sonar-project.properties:4 = sonar.sources=src; ci-quality.yml:2704-2715 critical_paths subset; ci-quality.yml:2688-2691 "Coverage effort is proportional to risk"; spec.md:29/50/56 quoted correctly. However, the finding's interpretation does not withstand scrutiny — it quotes the "fix Sonar sources/exclusions" clause and C-002's "file-set misconfiguration vs philosophy" while omitting the guardrails in the very same sentences and the mission's own framing.\n\nRefutation: (1) Edge-case-1 explicitly DEFINES the philosophy difference as "(whole-repo average vs per-PR-diff)" — which is precisely the Sonar-whole-src vs internal-critical-path situation the finding describes. The spec has already classified the observed difference as the philosophy bucket, whose deliverable is documentation, not a code change. The file-set difference is the footprint of the philosophy, not a separate misconfiguration category. (2) The "fix sources/exclusions" branch is triple-gated ("materially misconfigured" + "only if it is a genuine config bug" + "Do not force a code change where docs are the honest answer"), and FR-003 restates "config change only if a genuine misconfiguration (not a philosophy difference)". A by-design difference is by definition not a config bug. (3) The mission's Primary-actor text (spec.md:9) and US2 (spec.md:20-22) already frame the scope difference as an EXPECTED scope difference to be documented, not narrowed. (4) The exact harmful outcome the finding fears (narrowing sonar.sources to critical-path, suppressing whole-repo debt) is independently and explicitly prohibited by NFR-002 ("must not suppress, allowlist, or ratchet away any real SonarCloud finding") and SC-004 — both HIGH-priority and binding. So the recommended "explicit guard" is already present in substance.

dropped.summary: The 'reused' research grounding (Stijn's research.md) is absent from the repo, so FR-003 must be re-investigated and SC-002 cannot lean on it
dropped.why_refuted:
Refuted against live artifacts. The finding treats provenance/attribution language ("reuses Stijn's research.md decisions") as a load-bearing input dependency gating FR-003/SC-002, but the spec is deliberately self-grounding and the finding's own remedy is already the mission's design.

Substantive claims refuted by spec text: (1) "FR-003 must be re-investigated" — FR-003 (spec.md:41) and its Edge Case (spec.md:29) already define a fresh, research-first, IN-MISSION investigation with the exact method spelled out (GET /api/measures/component_tree scoped to coverage vs the internal gate's critical-path file list) and deliberately leave the conclusion open; the mission never delegated the investigation to research.md, so nothing needs re-investigation. (2) "SC-002 cannot lean on it [research.md]" — SC-002 (spec.md:68) explicitly reads "backed by the FR-003 investigation" (the in-mission one), reinforced by C-002 (spec.md:56). The finding's recommendation ("budget for a fresh FR-003 investigation… SC-002 must be backed by an in-mission investigation") is verbatim redundant with what the spec already mandates.

Self-grounding verified in the live repo: sonar.projectVersion is never set (grep of .github/workflows/ci-quality.yml + sonar-project.properties returns NONE) → FR-001 grounding is real and in-repo; the FR-003 comparison input (internal diff-coverage critical-path list) exists at ci-quality.yml:2704 and scripts/ci/quality_gate_decision.py exists → the in-mission investigation is executable without research.md; the DM-01KWV7EJ decision (pyproject.toml as version source) is restated inline (spec.md:30/:79/FR-001/FR-002). So the absent research.md is not a planner/implementer blocker.

Evidentiary error in the finding: its claim that "no provenance marker (…the branch name) appears anywhere outside spec.md" is FALSE — the branch name design/pr-landing-followups-and-sonar-remediation, the upstream mission id ci-hygiene-and-sonar-debt-remediation-01KWV531, and "research.md decisions" all appear in meta.json (purpose_context) AND in status.events.jsonl (the canonical MissionCreated event). The provenance is durably recorded in canonical mission metadata, not orphaned.

True-but-immaterial parts (upstream dir absent, research.md not vendored) support only a cosmetic provenance-hygiene nit (Key Entities lists an out-of-repo doc), which does not block planning/implementation — hence adjusted severity low rather than medium.

dropped.summary: Smoke-test CI placement unspecified; the only Sonar job is token-gated, so 'runs in CI with no SONAR_TOKEN' is not enforceable
dropped.why_refuted:
Factual claims are accurate (sonarcloud job at ci-quality.yml:2792 is the sole Sonar job, all steps gated on sonar_token.outputs.enabled at 2865/3130, only runs on schedule/dispatch, SONAR_TOKEN used only in this job), but the finding's reasoning does not withstand scrutiny.

(1) The smoke test is new Python (spec C-003:57 "ruff+mypy clean on new Python (the smoke test)"), i.e. a pytest test. Pytest runs via `uv run python -m pytest` in the fast-tests-*/integration-tests-* jobs, none of which reference SONAR_TOKEN and all of which run on the pull_request trigger. The sonarcloud scanner job runs NO pytest at all. So the natural, path-of-least-resistance home is a token-free PR job — the opposite of the finding's premise.

(2) The core inference ("the only Sonar job is token-gated, so 'runs in CI with no SONAR_TOKEN' is not enforceable") is a non-sequitur: it conflates the Sonar scanner job with the job that runs the smoke test. The scanner job's token-gating is irrelevant to smoke-test placement/enforceability.

(3) "No SONAR_TOKEN" is stated four times (Edge Cases:32, FR-006:44, NFR-001:49, SC-003:69) and unambiguously means the test must FUNCTION without the secret (public read endpoints or a recorded fixture) — a no-token dependency, not a job-placement mandate. The finding reinterprets it as a job-location requirement.

(4) The fakeability scenario actually VIOLATES the letter it claims to satisfy: a step gated `if sonar_token.enabled == 'true'` is skipped when no token is present, so it neither "runs in CI" (FR-006) nor "passes in CI with no SONAR_TOKEN" (SC-003). Hiding a pytest test inside a scanner-only job that only fires on schedule/dispatch is contrived and would fail plain-reading acceptance, not satisfy it.

(5) Stage mismatch: this is a spec-stage artifact (no plan.md; tasks/ has only README + .gitkeep). Naming the concrete CI job is a plan/implementation concern; the spec already constrains scripts/ci/ location, runs-in-CI, no-token, public-endpoint/fixture. The only residual value is a minor precision nit in the recommendation, which does not rescue the stated mechanism. Hence adjusted_severity low, real false.

dropped.summary: FR-003/SC-002 present a false dichotomy; the config shows file sets AND philosophy both differ, with no 'intentional difference' branch
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding does not withstand scrutiny against the live spec. It rests on a partial quote of spec.md:29 and a misread of SC-002.

(1) Misread routing: The finding says line 29 "routes 'file sets differ' toward 'materially misconfigured -> fix'." The actual text gates a fix behind two qualifiers it omits: "If the file sets are **materially misconfigured** -> document which files/dirs differ (and, **only if it is a genuine config bug**, fix the Sonar sources/exclusions). Do not force a code change where docs are the honest answer." The fix trigger is "materially misconfigured" + "genuine config bug," not merely "differ."

(2) The proposed "third state" (intentional difference -> document, not fix) is already present twice: line 29's closing sentence ("Do not force a code change where docs are the honest answer") and C-002 (line 56: "Do not write a code 'scope alignment' fix unless the investigation proves a genuine file-set misconfiguration; a philosophy-only difference is discharged by documentation"). An intentional, non-misconfigured file-set difference is explicitly routed to documentation.

(3) SC-002 bypass is contradicted by SC-002 itself: it requires the note to "**accurately state** whether the file sets differ or only the threshold philosophy does." In the repo reality (file sets do differ), a doc claiming "only philosophy differs" is inaccurate and FAILS SC-002 — the opposite of the finding's claim that such a doc could pass. The accurate, satisfiable answer for the both-differ case is the disjunction's first arm ("the file sets differ").

(4) The repo-reality claim is factually confirmed (sonar-project.properties:4 sonar.sources=src whole-repo vs ci-quality.yml:2704-2715/2738-2741 curated critical_paths diff-cover subset — both file set and philosophy differ), but it is non-load-bearing: the spec's investigation branch already treats "file sets genuinely differ" as a first-class case and gates any code change behind "genuine config bug," so the both-differ reality correctly lands on documentation, which is exactly the recommendation's desired outcome.

Residual value is only a minor optional enhancement — adding an objective, falsifiable misconfiguration test to sharpen "materially misconfigured." That is a nice-to-have wording refinement, not a "high, boundary" defect; the wrong-fix class it guards against is already blocked by C-002 and the "only if it is a genuine config bug" gate. Hence real=false; severity, if anything, low.

dropped.summary: Unaddressed tension: setting projectVersion resets the new-code baseline, reclassifying ~4 months of currently-'new' findings out of the gate — arguably in tension with NFR-002
dropped.why_refuted: Evidence line numbers are accurate (spec.md:17 US1 "resets per dev cycle instead of freezing"; spec.md:50 NFR-002 "must not suppress, allowlist, or ratchet away any real SonarCloud finding") and the mechanic (projectVersion reset reclassifies accumulated drift new→old) is directionally right. But the finding's core claim — that this is an UNADDRESSED tension the spec "never states acceptability of" under NFR-002 — is a misread and is already-handled: (1) NFR-002's own lead clause is the explicit carve-out: "This mission changes how the gate measures... it must not suppress, allowlist, or ratchet away any real SonarCloud finding" — the projectVersion fix IS the permitted measurement change; the prohibition targets removing a finding from existence, not re-scoping the new-code window. (2) spec.md:9 explicitly frames the CURRENT "all ~4 months = new" state as the DEFECT being fixed (it misattributes repo-wide drift to individual PRs), so the reclassification is the stated GOAL (US1:17, FR-001:39, SC-001:67), not a hidden side effect. (3) SC-004 (line 70) co-lists "No suppression/ratchet/allowlist (NFR-002)" WITH SC-001 (baseline reset) as jointly-satisfiable — the authors demonstrably do not view reset as masking. (4) Edge case line 30 shows they deliberately reasoned about baseline-reset semantics and accepted them. (5) Substantively no contradiction exists: a reclassified finding remains in Sonar's overall-code measures — it is not suppressed/allowlisted/removed, so NFR-002 is factually not implicated; the "tension" is a surface word-association between "shrinking the window" and "ratchet." (6) Both halves of the recommendation are already in the spec — the "intended #2421 effect" (lines 9/17/39/67) and "separate tracking under epic #1928" for the accumulated backlog (lines 4-5, C-001:55, Out-of-Scope:73), which the finding even cites itself. No plan.md/research.md exists (research/ empty), so spec.md is authoritative and it resolves the concern internally. Residual value is at most a one-line clarification co-located in NFR-002 (low-value polish), which does not meet the bar of a medium drift defect. Under "default to real=false unless it clearly withstands scrutiny," it does not withstand.

dropped.summary: US2/SC-002 acceptance rests on a subjective 'a reader can correctly classify' with no concrete pass condition
dropped.why_refuted:
Adversarial verdict: the finding does NOT withstand scrutiny — it rests on a misread of SC-002 and ignores the objective, empirically-anchored clause plus the mechanical check that already exist in the spec.

Quote accuracy (partial): The US2 line is quoted verbatim. spec.md:22 (/home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2421/kitty-specs/sonar-qa-config-remediation-01KWYCX7/spec.md) reads: "given the documented explanation, a reader can correctly classify an apparent Sonar-vs-internal discrepancy as either a real regression or an expected scope difference." Confirmed.

The load-bearing claim is FALSE. The finding asserts "spec.md:68 SC-002 repeats this" and that acceptance rests solely on a subjective 'a reader can correctly classify' with "no concrete pass condition" and "no ... mechanical check." SC-002 (line 68) does not merely repeat the US2 test — it conjoins a second, objective, falsifiable clause: "...it accurately states whether the file sets differ or only the threshold philosophy does (backed by the FR-003 investigation)." That clause is a concrete pass condition: a doc that states the wrong answer FAILS SC-002, so it is emphatically NOT true that "any prose doc can be asserted to satisfy it." The doc's factual claim is checked against ground truth, not against a reader's subjective interpretation.

A mechanical check IS specified, contradicting the finding. FR-003 (line 41) and the Edge Cases block (line 29) prescribe the decision procedure: "via the unauthenticated GET /api/measures/component_tree (scoped to coverage) vs the internal gate's critical-path file list, whether the file sets genuinely differ or only the threshold philosophy (whole-repo average vs per-PR-diff) differs." C-002 (line 56) further binds this as a research-first constraint. So the acceptance chain has empirical teeth: an API-derived Sonar coverage file-set compared against the internal critical-path list.

The anchor sets the finding's own recommendation asks for already exist and are concrete/verified against live artifacts: sonar.sources=src in /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2421/sonar-project.properties (line 4), and the internal critical_paths=( ... ) array in /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2421/.github/workflows/ci-quality.yml (lines 2704-2715). These are materially different scopes (whole src tree vs a curated critical-path subset), so SC-002's "accurately states whether the file sets differ" is an objectively determinable claim against real data — exactly the kind of anchored, checkable condition the finding claims is absent.

Category error: the finding conflates "a human reviewer verifies the doc" with "subjective." Verifying that the doc's factual determination matches the empirical FR-003 investigation (file-sets-differ vs philosophy-only) is an objective check against ground truth, not a subjective read.

Residual value: the recommendation to add two worked cases (one genuine regression, one expected-scope difference) is a reasonable nice-to-have hardening, but the finding's stated defect — no concrete pass condition, any prose passes, no mechanical check — is factually refuted by SC-002's second clause + FR-003 + the Edge Cases procedure. Because the core premise misreads the artifact, the finding is not real. If retained at all it would be a low-severity optional-enhancement note, not a medium testability defect.
